nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; inhabits coastal and offshore rocky reefs, drop-offs, caves, crevices, and ledges. Generally reported at depths of 3–45 m, most commonly 15–25 m.

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on small fish and crustaceans in the wild. In captivity, generally takes meaty foods such as silversides, krill, chopped shrimp, squid, and mysis; live saltwater feeder shrimp are often used to entice newly introduced specimens to feed. Typically fed 2–4 times weekly once acclimated, reflecting the species' relatively slow metabolism and ambush-feeding style.

summary: The Western (or Southern) Blue Devil, Paraplesiops meleagris, is a striking cave-dwelling roundhead endemic to temperate southern Australian reefs, generally advised for experienced keepers with large, cool-water systems and abundant rockwork.
careNotes:
The Western Blue Devil is a deep-bodied, long-finned plesiopid endemic to the temperate reefs of southern Australia, from Western Australia through South Australia and occasionally to western Victoria. It is strongly associated with caves, crevices, and undercut ledges, and tends to be cryptic and nocturnal — most active at dusk and through the night, sheltering in rockwork during the day. Dim lighting or shaded refuges are generally advised so the fish will venture out to feed.

This is a temperate-water species. Sources cluster around 15–24°C: LiveAquaria recommends 18.3–23.9°C (65–75°F), FishBase reports an environmental range of 18–22°C, Reeflex lists 15.1–20.9°C, and Fish Index cites 17.8–22.2°C. The averaged range of 17–22°C has been recorded here. A chiller is commonly needed in warmer climates; sustained tropical temperatures above ~25°C have been reported to be stressful long-term.

Regarding tank size, sources disagreed considerably — LiveAquaria recommends 180 US gallons (~681 L), Reeflex recommends at least 500 L, and one hobbyist source suggests as little as 70 US gallons (~265 L). Given the species reaches around 30 cm, is strongly territorial, naturally forms breeding pairs with strong site fidelity around a particular cave, and benefits from extensive rockwork, the larger end has been favored here and rounded to 700 L. A long footprint and substantial structured rockwork with multiple cave systems are generally advised over depth.

Despite being marketed as 'peaceful' by some retailers, this species is best treated as territorial: it has been known to eat fish and shrimp small enough to swallow, can be aggressive toward conspecifics outside of bonded pairs, and is highly site-attached. Generally advised to be kept singly or as an established mated pair, and not mixed with very small fish or ornamental shrimp.

Reef compatibility is 'with caution': it will not typically nip corals but will hunt mobile invertebrates and small fishes. Lifespan in the wild is reported to be extremely long for a reef fish — aging studies on this species have documented individuals from roughly 7 to 42 years, with one male estimated at 59 years. A more conservative captive expectation of around 8–20+ years is used here.

Field-gap suggestion: this entry would benefit from an explicit 'temperateRequired' or 'coldWaterSpecies' flag, as the temperate temperature requirement is a major husbandry distinction from typical tropical marine fish.
breedingNotes: Forms long-term breeding pairs in the wild, with eggs guarded by the male parent (paternal mouthbrooding is not documented; demersal egg-guarding is). Captive breeding has rarely been reported in home aquaria, in part due to the large temperate system required to house a pair. Generally considered difficult.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary care sheet. Recommends 180 gal minimum, max size 13 inches (~33 cm), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, temperature 65–75°F, care level moderate, reef compatibility 'with caution', diet carnivore.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Plesiopidae), max length (33 cm TL), depth (3–45 m, usually 15–25 m), native distribution (WA and SA), environment temperature (18–22°C), paternal egg-guarding behavior, IUCN Least Concern. Direct URL initially 403'd; resolved via fishbase.se mirror.

additional.name: Fishes of Australia
additional.notes: Confirmed distribution (Port Phillip, VIC to Exmouth, WA), depth (3–45 m), habitat (coastal and offshore reefs, caves and overhangs), breeding-pair social structure with strong site fidelity, and exceptional longevity (aged 7–42 years in studies; oldest individual 59 years).

additional.name: Reeflex
additional.notes: Cross-checked aquarium care: 500 L minimum, 15.1–20.9°C, max length 22–25 cm (smaller than other sources), difficulty 'average', diet carnivore on small fish and crustaceans. Tank-size estimate factored toward the larger LiveAquaria figure.

additional.name: Fish Index
additional.notes: Cross-checked nocturnal/territorial behavior, betta-like extended fins, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–12, SG 1.020–1.025, temperature 64–72°F. Notes the 'Blue Betta' trade name.

fish.breedingNotes: Forms breeding pairs with strong site fidelity in the wild; eggs are guarded by the male. Rarely bred in home aquaria.
